channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mittens the cat becomes an accidental frequent flyer after getting mistakenly left on a plane
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing the risk of sending kids to school
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump Administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page, social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, his administration took down the Spanish version of the official White House website.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fire risk and strong winds continue in Southern California with potential rain on the horizon
item.description: Parched Southern California could get some badly needed rain this weekend to dampen the prospects of another round of killer wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GOP-led states are emboldened to keep rolling back trans rights. Democrats struggle with a response
item.description: Republicans in statehouses are emboldened by GOP electoral successes to push a new wave of bills to restrict transgender rights and gender-affirming care.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, John Hanna And Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Diddy accuser: I didn't have the 'guts' for immediate rape test
item.description: "I didn't have the guts to go earlier, and I regret that," Parham said during an exclusive interview on NewsNation's "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans renew efforts to limit people in US illegally from census count
item.description: Republican efforts to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states have begun anew, with four Republican state attorneys general suing to alter the once-a-decade head count even before President Donald Trump’s second term in office began.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look to find favorable jury as trial begins on charges he fired on ex-friend
item.description: Nearly 100 jurors will pack into a Los Angeles courtroom on Wednesday with the possibility they’ll serve on the three-week trial of rapper A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dalton, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kentucky sheriff accused of killing judge makes court appearance from jail
item.description: A former Kentucky sheriff accused of gunning down a judge last year, stunning their tight-knit Appalachian community, appeared remotely from a jail Friday for a court hearing as defense attorneys and prosecutors discussed preliminary matters that will lead up to his murder trial.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombians flee to Venezuela as clashes between rebel groups escalate in coca-rich border region
item.description: TRES BOCAS, Colombia (AP) — The Colombian border village of Tres Bocas has become a ghost town as residents flee to neighboring Venezuela to escape a new wave of violence in Colombia’s Catatumbo region that has left at least 80 people dead and displaced thousands.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDO VERGARA and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont near Canada
item.description: Authorities on Tuesday were investigating a fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent that also left a suspect dead and another injured on a Vermont highway near Canada, authorities said. Agent David Maland was killed Monday afternoon following a traffic stop, a U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California officials reopen certain Palisades Fire zones, cops to use planes to monitor looting
item.description: California officials will reopen some Palisades Fire evacuation zones, as law enforcement ramps up security to address looting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump defends pardoning of violent Jan. 6 offenders
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday defended signing mass pardons for rioters charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including those who were violent offenders, a move that has drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security Tuesday, firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up and eliminated all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump wants to make showerheads and toilets flow greatly again, but so may utility bills
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump once expressed concern with how low showerhead flow affected his “perfect” hair. Now back in the White House, he's again taking aim at some high-efficiency household items — and that may mean higher water and electric bills in your home.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Nose spray to treat depression? FDA approves first-of-its-kind medicine
item.description: The main ingredient for the spray is esketamine hydrochloride, which is the "chemical cousin" of ketamine.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: California wildfires: Suspect arrested in connection with brush fire quickly quashed by firefighters
item.description: Los Angeles authorities have arrested a man after a brush fire broke out in the Griffith Park neighborhood on Monday afternoon, according to the LAPD.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KRON4

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump goes toe-to-toe with sanctuary cities over deportation as illegal immigration crackdown set to begin
item.description: Sanctuary cities, like Chicago and San Diego, have doubled down on their commitment to be a safe haven for migrants and refuse to assist in federal deportation efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay Area tech CEOs get front-row seats at Trump inauguration
item.description: (KRON) -- The biggest tech leaders of Silicon Valley got a front-row seat at President Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday. There has been a sudden support this administration has gained from the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Sara Stinson, KRON4

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: 250 roosters, guns, cash seized in Southern California cockfighting bust
item.description: (KTLA) - Authorities in San Bernardino County arrested two men, detained 33 others and seized more than 200 fighting roosters, U.S. currency and dozens of firearms, officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio State wins 1st national title since 2014, outlasting Notre Dame 34-23 in CFP championship game
item.description: Instead of crying over another collapse, Ohio State can celebrate another national title after holding off a Notre Dame comeback bid Monday night to walk away with a nailbiter of a 34-23 victory over the Fighting Irish.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire crews in Southern California quickly extinguish brush fires amid extreme fire weather
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters quickly extinguished several brush fires that erupted Monday in Southern California amid windy and dry conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ramaswamy won't serve on Trump's DOGE as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the Republican nomination for president in 2024, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Longtime Planned Parenthood head Cecile Richards dies at 67
item.description: Cecile Richards, the former president of Planned Parenthood, died at the age of 67 on Monday after a battle with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes sentence of Native American activist Leonard Peltier
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday commuted the life sentence of activist Leonard Peltier, who was controversially convicted of murdering two FBI agents, within the final hour of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KRON4

item.title: MLK Jr.'s daughter: 'Wonderful' Trump being inaugurated on day celebrating father
item.description: Bernice King, the daughter of late civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr., says the simultaneous occurrence of MLK Day and Inauguration Day this year is an opportunity to reflect on her father's legacy.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, ABC 4
